description_templates: a derelict structure on the edge of the district, windows long since shattered
floors warped with moisture, walls stained with something that predates the abandonment
the kind of place that attracts curses the way rot attracts flies
tactical_notes: the curse has likely been here for a while and may have established a territory
the building's instability creates environmental hazards but also opportunities for creative technique usage
civilians are unlikely to enter the building, but nearby residents may call the authorities if they hear disturbances

description_templates: an alleyway tucked between two massive buildings in a crowded Tokyo district
dumpsters sit a respectable distance from the sidewalk, accessible through both ends of the alleys and doors to adjoining buildings
broken glass and wooden crates line the sides of the alley at its deepest points
tactical_notes: civilians are unlikely to enter the alleyway to investigate sounds, but may call in the authorities
multiple entry points make containment difficult
open air offers vertical means of escape for errant curses

description_templates: a cellar below the estate of a now-diminished clan, containing a variety of cursed weapons, tools, and artifacts from across the centuries
a set of thick wooden doors in the estate basement lead into a veritable museum of artifacts, each carrying its own unique cursed energy signature
a store-room in the back of the house has been converted into a cursed artifact repository, leading family members to avoid this part of the house
tactical_notes: collateral damage to other cursed items can easily occur; care must be taken to avoid this
the remaining clan members who own this property may be protective of the artifacts here; do not anger them
virtually no risk of civilian interference

description_templates: a public elementary school gone quiet after hours, small desks and crayon drawings an uncomfortable contrast to what's taken up residence in the building
a three-story school building in a residential neighborhood, the kind of place that parents pass every day without sensing what's been growing inside it
classrooms locked for the weekend, corridors lit only by emergency lighting — the curse has been feeding on the residual fear of children for weeks
tactical_notes: small furniture and low ceilings restrict movement during physical confrontations
proximity to residential areas means any visible technique usage risks witness exposure
cursed spirits here often take forms shaped by childhood fear — unpredictable and emotionally charged
daytime operations are effectively impossible due to civilian presence

description_templates: a large public high school on the edge of the district, its gymnasium and rooftop accessible after hours through a broken fire exit
a school building that has been reporting incidents for months — staff attribute it to stress, students know better
three floors of classrooms, a basement gym, and a rooftop with a clear sightline across the surrounding neighborhood
tactical_notes: rooftop access provides elevated terrain advantage
gymnasium offers the most open space for technique usage
student population is partially curse-aware due to proximity — risk of exposure higher than a grade school
school records may contain clues about the curse's origin and feeding patterns

description_templates: a mid-size general hospital operating at capacity, staff stretched thin on the night shift
sterile corridors that feel anything but safe once you sense what's moving through the walls
the concentration of fear and grief makes it a natural incubator for cursed spirits
tactical_notes: civilians are unlikely to enter the hospital to investigate sounds, but may call in the authorities
multiple entry points make containment difficult
the curse has likely been feeding on the fear and grief of patients and staff — expect emotionally charged confrontations with any survivors

description_templates: a budget hostel where three guests have gone missing in two weeks — management filed a noise complaint with the wrong authorities
a six-floor hostel in a tourist district, its communal kitchen and rooftop lounge now abandoned by guests who sense something is wrong without being able to name it
a hostel operating at half capacity after online reviews began mentioning nightmares, sleepwalking, and guests who checked out without collecting their deposits
tactical_notes: transient civilian population is harder to evacuate cleanly than a fixed residential building
shared bathrooms and dormitory rooms create ambush-friendly terrain
international guests may not respond appropriately to evacuation instructions
the curse has likely been feeding on sleep — guests may be weakened or disoriented

description_templates: a garish establishment tucked between two larger buildings, its vacancy sign flickering
narrow corridors and mirrored walls create a disorienting maze of identical rooms
guests have been reporting disturbances for weeks — management assumed noise complaints
tactical_notes: cursed spirits here sometimes have a sexual aspect that can be psychologically destabilizing for victims
the layout creates ample opportunity for ambushes and traps
the curse has likely been feeding on sexual energy — victims may be disoriented or emotionally compromised

description_templates: a utility room buried inside the station's service infrastructure, its walls lined with fuse boxes and pipe junctions — access requires a key that facilities management reported missing three months ago
a cramped mechanical room beneath a commercial building, its floor vibrating with the hum of industrial equipment that hasn't been serviced in years

a former security monitoring room, its screens still active but showing only static — whatever is in here has been interfering with the electronics long enough that management stopped sending technicians
tactical_notes: industrial equipment creates unpredictable interference with cursed energy sensing
extremely confined space heavily disadvantages technique-reliant sorcerers
single entry point makes the room easy to seal but difficult to escape
electrical infrastructure is a hazard if compromised during combat

description_templates: a modern art museum filled with displays, both sculpture and two dimensional; the white marble walls and floor offer plentiful opportunity to take noticeable damage, and the artwork here is valuable
a history museum full of artifacts from across the centuries, including displays showcasing a variety of cultures, both Japanese and foreign; these are priceless artifacts in some cases

a museum celebrating Japan's past, full of colorful depictions of life from the Heian through the Edo periods; the museum is renowned for hosting concerts showcasing traditional Japanese music and theater. It is closed for the day, supposedly to fix a plumbing issue
tactical_notes: valuable artifacts are everywhere, making precision invaluable when dealing with any curses here
rooms are separated by large partitions and many-doored corridors, making it a veritable maze. Curses can easily hide here

description_templates: a mid-size club wedged into a basement level, its entrance marked only by a bouncer and a velvet rope — the music is loud enough to mask screaming
three floors of darkness, strobing light, and bodies pressed together — the kind of place where someone could disappear between one song and the next
an upscale lounge operating on two levels, the upper floor reserved for private booths where the city's wealthy conduct business they'd rather not document
tactical_notes: bass and crowd noise mask technique activation sounds
strobing lights interfere with visual tracking of fast-moving curses
multiple emergency exits complicate perimeter control
intoxicated civilians are slower to evacuate and more likely to panic

description_templates: a late-night platform reduced to a handful of waiting commuters, the train board counting down in silence — whatever is here has been feeding on stragglers for weeks
a disused platform sealed off from the public network, accessible only through a rusted service door — the tracks still run through it but no train has stopped here in years
an active platform during off-peak hours, its fluorescent lighting casting everything in a flat white that makes shadows look wrong
tactical_notes: live rail lines present a constant environmental hazard
platform edges create dangerous terrain during physical confrontations
disused platforms offer better containment but attract stronger entrenched spirits
train schedules create windows of civilian absence that can be tactically exploited

description_templates: a section of Tokyo's drainage network accessible through a maintenance hatch in a quiet side street — the smell hits before the darkness does
a decommissioned overflow tunnel running beneath the district, wide enough to stand in but low enough to feel the ceiling at every turn
a branching network of drainage channels beneath a residential ward, dry in most sections but flooded in others — the curse has been using the water to move
tactical_notes: standing water interferes with cursed energy tracking and technique precision
the enclosed geometry amplifies sound — a curse can be heard approaching long before it arrives
branching tunnels create disorientation risk even for experienced sorcerers
the absence of civilians allows for unrestricted technique usage
cursed spirits here tend toward the aquatic and the ancient — long-established and territorial

description_templates: a mid-size shopping mall closed for the evening, its atrium lit only by emergency strips and the glow of darkened storefronts
a four-floor retail complex with an underground food court and a rooftop parking structure — after hours, the space becomes something else entirely
a mall that has been reporting equipment malfunctions, escalator accidents, and one incident that security cameras somehow failed to capture
tactical_notes: multi-level atrium offers vertical combat terrain but risks structural damage
store interiors provide cover but limit sightlines significantly
after hours operations reduce civilian risk substantially
the curse has likely established a central territory — locating it first is tactically essential

description_templates: a skyscraper construction site in the heart of the district, its skeletal frame looming over the streets below — workers have been reporting tools disappearing and strange noises at night
a high-rise under construction, surrounded by a fence but otherwise open to the city — the curse has been feeding on the fear of workers who stay late into the night to finish their shifts
a construction site with a half-finished building, its exposed steel beams and concrete floors creating a maze of cover and elevation
tactical_notes: the open structure allows for vertical combat but risks collateral damage to the building framework
construction equipment can be both a hazard and an opportunity during combat
the stairs are constructed, but the elevator shaft is not — this creates a dangerous vertical drop that can be exploited by curses or unwary sorcerers
civilians are unlikely to enter the site after hours, but nearby pedestrians may call in disturbances

description_templates: a maintenance corridor running beneath the district, unlisted on any public map — the walls sweat with moisture and the air tastes of rust and stagnant water
a decommissioned utility tunnel, its original purpose long forgotten, now home to whatever finds its way down from the streets above
a stretch of unmapped passage beneath the station network, narrow enough that two people cannot pass each other without turning sideways
tactical_notes: total darkness beyond flashlight range — cursed energy perception becomes the primary sense
confined geometry makes large-scale technique usage dangerous to the user
sound carries unpredictably — a curse can be heard long before it is located
single entry and exit points are tactically advantageous but create escape risks

description_templates: a large, industrial warehouse that sees little use at night; it smells faintly of petroleum and rust
a small, abandoned warehouse tucked between a larger warehouse and a factory; it feels out of place between the behemoths beside it
a newly renovated warehouse with clean concrete floors and whitewashed walls; it's mostly unused at the moment, offering improved mobility and vision
tactical_notes: echoing area can be disorienting, which some curses use to their advantage
in cramped quarters like these, collateral damage to property is almost unavoidable
it's not well lit, meaning there are many dark corners for curses to hide in
